commit_scope: Release the hot empty household events read from Python fanout into a BML front-door route. Production currently has no configured iCal feeds and returns an empty list; the new native route preserves that public contract as [] while adding native proof headers and leaving full external iCal source-port ingestion as a future Form lift.

local_validation.notes: Prompt guide and wellness passed. Public production before the change returned HTTP 200 with an empty JSON list for /api/household/events?limit=5. Focused provenance tests passed (6 passed). The fresh Rust release build succeeded. Local BML serve listed 40 native routes including GET /api/household/events. The local normal request returned HTTP 200, X-Form-Router: native-kernel, X-Form-Handler: api_household_events, X-Form-Python-Authority: false, and body=[]. The invalid limit request returned native HTTP 422 with a JSON detail. The rebased route-goal read moved observed high-grammar share from 74.85% before the route to 92.20% after the route, with /api/household/events classified as kernel-native-high-grammar and no promotion route required for the 90% window target. The local PR guard and stale-followthrough check passed.

known_failed_attempts.output: check reported pre-existing unresolved manifest names such as str_line_at, source_compile_last_error, compile_source_text, JSON-OBJECT, and older framebuffer-observe names.
known_failed_attempts.resolution: Used the deploy-equivalent serve path instead. The same manifest compiled and served successfully, listing GET /api/household/events as native, and live curls proved the new handler.

ci_validation.notes: Remote CI is pending until this branch is pushed and a PR is opened.

deploy_validation.notes: Public deploy verification now includes GET /api/household/events native proof headers and empty-list body contract.

phase_gate.notes: Local proof passed. Rebase, guard, commit, push, PR, CI, merge, deploy, and public verification remain.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Current no-calendar household events reads stop crossing the Python membrane. The response body remains the same empty list, while the route is served by native BML and carries native proof headers.
test_flows: The BML route returns X-Form-Handler: api_household_events and X-Form-Python-Authority: false.
GET /api/household/events?limit=0 returns a native 422 instead of silently accepting an invalid limit.
scripts/verify_web_api_deploy.sh and deploy/hostinger/auto-deploy.sh both fail unless this route is native and keeps the empty-calendar contract.

evidence_refs: deploy/kernel-router/docker-compose.kernel-router.yml routes GET /api/household/events to the BML front-door service.
deploy/hostinger/auto-deploy.sh probes the local BML front door for native route headers and [] body.
scripts/verify_web_api_deploy.sh checks the public native route headers and empty-list body.
api/tests/test_runtime_web_api_provenance.py asserts the route-goal loop sees GET /api/household/events as kernel-native high-grammar.
